channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Export historical data from Log Analytics workspace with Export Job (preview)
item.description: Log Analytics Export Job is now available in public preview. It gives you a straightforward way to export historical log data from your workspace to Azure Blob Storage, without writing custom scripts or disrupting live operations. You submit a job including a query, time range, and the service...
item.dc:creator: YossiY

item.title: What’s New in Microsoft Foundry | June 2026
item.description: Claude is now generally available in Microsoft Foundry. Here's everything else that shipped between Build 2026 and the end of June — autopilot agents, expanded Toolboxes and Routines, Agent Optimizer's private preview, and more. The post What’s New in Microsoft Foundry | June 2026 appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Nick Brady

item.title: Introducing Kimi K2.7 Code in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Modern software engineering rarely happens in a single step, developers must refactor across large codebases, implement features spanning multiple files, and debug issues over long-running sessions. Kimi K2.7 Code from Moonshot AI, available on Microsoft Foundry starting July 1, 2026 in public...
item.dc:creator: RashaudSavage

item.title: See our new Azure Cosmos DB Design Patterns
item.description: Design patterns are where good data modeling lives or dies. In a NoSQL database like Azure Cosmos DB, the difference between a schema that scales to millions of operations per second and one that fights you at every turn usually comes down to a handful of well‑understood patterns: how you...
item.dc:creator: Mark Brown

item.title: 5 insights from Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar™ for Cloud Security Posture Management
item.description: Read five key learnings from the Frost & Sullivan 2025 Frost Radar™ for CSPM to learn how CSPM is evolving from point-in-time compliance to continuous risk management. The post 5 insights from Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar™ for Cloud Security Posture Management appeared first on Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Security Team

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now generally available in all regions. You can now use Entra ID identities including guest users via Entra External Identities to securely connect to Azure Blob Storage over SFTP, without pro
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Support 5x churn in Azure Site Recovery
item.description: Azure Site Recovery now supports up to 5x churn (500 MB/s per VM). This major enhancement empowers customers to confidently run high IOPS workloads with Azure Site Recovery, ensuring robust disaster recovery for even the most demanding applications like d
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Logic Apps Aviators Newsletter - July 2026
item.description: In this issue: Ace Aviator of the Month News from our product group News from our community Ace Aviator of the Month July 2026's Ace Aviator: Bill Chesnut Bill Chesnut - Cloud Platform and API Evangelist @ SixPivot What's your role and title? What are your responsibilities? Cloud Platform and API...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: v1.16.0-alpha20260706
item.description: 1.16.0-alpha20260706 (July 06, 2026) NEW FEATURES: Store PlannedPrivate data for providers (#37986) New store block in terraform_data that can handle ephemeral and sensitive values (#38298) Providers can now use nested blocks as computed values (#38305) import: add support for import blocks inside...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Securing message brokers takes more than turning off public access
item.description: Over the last few years, enterprises have done the hard work of locking down their cloud networks. Public endpoints get switched off. Traffic moves onto private IPs inside a virtual network. Shared secrets get replaced with identities that can be governed and revoked. This is the practical shape of...
item.dc:creator: EldertGrootenboer

item.title: Need a different partition key in Azure Cosmos DB? Pick the right approach
item.description: Once you create a container, its partition key is fixed at creation, and you can’t change it in place. However, if your original key starts causing problems like cross-partition queries or hot partitions, you need to consider your options for changing it. This post explains the mechanics of...
item.dc:creator: Abhishek Gupta

item.title: Token Economics: The New FinOps for Agentic AI
item.description: In AI applications, tokens are now cost — and token economics deserves architectural attention For a long time, AI application design started with model capability: Can the model write code? Can it reason? Can it use tools? Can it handle long context? Those questions still matter, but in the age of...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: Azure SDK Release (June 2026)
item.description: Azure SDK releases every month. In this post, you'll find this month's highlights and release notes. The post Azure SDK Release (June 2026) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Justin Bettencourt

item.title: How Prompt Tuning Improved GPT-5.5 in VS Code
item.description: See how VS Code and OpenAI tested GPT-5.5 system prompt changes in a two-week experiment, cutting tool calls and tail-end token usage while speeding up edits. Read the full article
item.dc:creator: VS Code Team

item.title: Automatically Route Azure Service Health Alerts to the Right Service Owners Using Agentic Logic Apps
item.description: As organizations adopt more Azure services, operational ownership naturally becomes distributed across multiple teams. The platform team may own Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), the database team may manage Azure SQL, while application teams are responsible for App Services and Logic Apps. Azure...
item.dc:creator: Arpit_MSFT

item.title: Azure landing zone (ALZ) enters its next chapter
item.description: Hey all 👋 I’ve got a genuinely exciting update to share today, and it’s one that’s been a long time coming: Azure landing zone (ALZ) is now an official Microsoft product, owned by the Azure Migrate product team. A bit of history first For the past five or so years, ALZ has been built completely in...
item.dc:creator: jtracey93msft

item.title: Enabling MLflow OpenAI Autolog on PySpark Workers
item.description: When distributing LLM calls across PySpark workers via mapInPandas, MLflow autolog silently fails. Here is how to fix it. The post Enabling MLflow OpenAI Autolog on PySpark Workers appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jaya Kumar

item.title: On the road to .NET 10 Support: Logic Apps Migration from In-Proc to Out-of-Proc hosting model
item.description: We will begin this migration in the coming weeks. For most customers, the change will be automatic and require no action. However, some apps will need customer updates before they can move to the new hosting model. This exception is: Logic Apps that use the current NuGet-based deployment model If...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Improved accuracy and coverage in Copilot usage metrics reports
item.description: We’ve made three improvements to the Copilot usage metrics API that make its reports more complete and accurate: GitHub Copilot CLI now reports suggested lines of code, users seen only… The post Improved accuracy and coverage in Copilot usage metrics reports appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Upcoming deprecation of Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash
item.description: We will deprecate Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on July 31st,… The post Upcoming deprecation of Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally available: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 on Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 through Azure Databricks AI Model Serving. Claude Sonnet 5 is Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet model to date, delivering near-Opus-level intelligence with Sonnet's cost efficiency and speed. Designed
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Copilot CLI no longer needs a personal access token in GitHub Actions
item.description: You can now run GitHub Copilot CLI in GitHub Actions using the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN. This means that you no longer need to create and store a personal access token (PAT),… The post Copilot CLI no longer needs a personal access token in GitHub Actions appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Customer Newsletter
item.description: What's new in Defender for Cloud? Microsoft Defender for Open-Source Relational Databases is now generally available for Amazon Web Services Relational Database Service (AWS RDS) instances. Receive database threat protection and sensitive data discovery insights for supported open-source relational...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Copilot agent session streaming is now in public preview
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Cloud customers with enterprise managed users can now access GitHub Copilot agent session data across all Copilot clients, including: Cloud agents operating on github.com and data resident deployments… The post Copilot agent session streaming is now in public preview appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Cost centers now support AI credit pools
item.description: You can now cap how much of your enterprise’s monthly included AI credits a cost center can use. This is available through the REST API today. Management in the cost… The post Cost centers now support AI credit pools appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: How GitHub used secret scanning to reach inbox zero
item.description: GitHub had 20,000+ secret scanning alerts across 15,000 repositories. Here's how we separated signal from noise, built remediation workflows, and reached inbox zero in nine months. The post How GitHub used secret scanning to reach inbox zero appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Improving security posture across the Microsoft partner ecosystem
item.description: Read how Microsoft strengthens partner ecosystem security with CSP vetting, least privilege access, monitoring, and risk management best practices. The post Improving security posture across the Microsoft partner ecosystem appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Raji Dani

item.title: Available today: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 in Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Starting today, Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 is available in Microsoft 365 Copilot—a new frontier model rolling out in Copilot Cowork and PowerPoint, built for agentic, multi-step work across everyday work: documents, spreadsheets, and presentation tasks. Anthropic describes Sonnet 5 as a more...
item.dc:creator: Bas Brekelmans

item.title: Sales Agent is now generally available: Bringing customer and deal intelligence into the flow of work
item.description: Give every seller the customer and deal context they need, wherever work happens—helping them prepare faster, follow up sooner, and keep CRM data current. Built for the pace of modern selling, Sales Agent helps sellers quickly access the customer and deal information they need, so they can enter...
item.dc:creator: gurkansalk

item.title: Foundry Control Plane and Agent 365: Two Control Planes Walk Into an Enterprise
item.description: May 15, 2026 | 8 min read If you’ve been paying attention to Microsoft over the past six months, you’ve noticed a recurring theme: agents are everywhere. They’re reasoning over your SharePoint folders, reconciling your accounts, and if you squint at the Ignite 2025 keynote, they’re about three...
item.dc:creator: Anil_Korkut

item.title: Fundamentals of Azure DevOps with SQL projects
item.description: Building automated pipelines with your SQL database projects enables you to build a rich CI/CD ecosystem to ensure that your application is being deployed with good quality code and at high confidence of success. SQL Database Projects are compatible with just about every automation environment...
item.dc:creator: Drew Skwiers-Koballa

item.title: Auditing and Telemetry for the Agent Governance Toolkit - Getting Started with .NET Core
item.description: Introduction We've entered an era where AI agents autonomously invoke tools — reading and writing files, calling APIs, querying databases. Convenient as this is, without a mechanism to control who can call what, and under what conditions, you can't put it into production. The Agent Governance...
item.dc:creator: daisami

item.title: Find anomalies in Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metrics with Dynamic Thresholds (Preview)
item.description: Dynamic thresholds are extended to query-based metric alerts in Azure Monitor, allowing to detect and alert on anomalies in Azure Monitor managed Prometheus metrics and OpenTelemetry metrics stored in an Azure Monitor Workspace. This follows the introduction of Dynamic Thresholds for Log search...
item.dc:creator: yairgil

item.title: Guest Access for Canvas and Model-Driven Apps with Microsoft Entra ID
item.description: Introduction External collaboration is a common requirement for business applications built on Microsoft Power Platform. Organizations may need to provide controlled access to vendors, partners, contractors, or external business users without creating full internal user accounts. Microsoft Entra ID...
item.dc:creator: aakarshdhawan

item.title: Issue fields are now generally available
item.description: Issue fields are now generally available for all GitHub organizations on Free, Team, Enterprise, and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency plans and will ship in GitHub Enterprise Server 3.23.… The post Issue fields are now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises
item.description: GitHub is committed to empowering the developer community by helping organizations recognize and address the risks of secret leaks wherever they happen. We believe every enterprise should know the moment… The post Secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Upcoming Change: NTLM Removal in Git (libcurl) – Impact to Azure DevOps Server Customers
item.description: Overview In September 2026, NTLM support will be removed from libcurl, which is used by Git for HTTP(S) operations. As a result, Git operations over HTTPS against Azure DevOps Server (on-premises) will stop working for customers who rely on NTLM authentication. This change is part of a broader...
item.dc:creator: Gloridel Morales

item.title: Enterprises can default to auto model selection
item.description: Enterprise administrators can now set model to auto in the enterprise managed-settings.json to make Copilot auto model selection the default for new conversations. Add auto to .github-private/.github/copilot/managed-settings.json in your source… The post Enterprises can default to auto model...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Models is being fully retired on July 30, 2026
item.description: In June, we announced that we are retiring GitHub Models and closed it to new customers. We’re now sharing the timeline for the next step: GitHub Models will be fully… The post GitHub Models is being fully retired on July 30, 2026 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Enterprise managed-settings.json is generally available
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Cloud customers can configure AI standards through a managed-settings.json file maintained in a .github-private repository in a selected organization. This allows the enterprise to define new governance and… The post Enterprise managed-settings.json is generally available appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Audit Frontier AI Agents with SQL MCP Server
item.description: With On-Behalf-Of authentication, SQL MCP Server lets agents access Microsoft SQL without losing the user identity behind the request. Azure SQL can audit the signed-in user who invoked the operation, not just the agent, app, or MCP server that carried it out. The post Audit Frontier AI Agents with...
item.dc:creator: Jerry Nixon

item.title: v1.16.0-alpha20260701
item.description: 1.16.0-alpha20260701 (July 01, 2026) NEW FEATURES: Store PlannedPrivate data for providers (#37986) New store block in terraform_data that can handle ephemeral and sensitive values (#38298) Providers can now use nested blocks as computed values (#38305) import: add support for import blocks inside...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Behind the Build with Gigamon: Enriching Microsoft Sentinel with Network-Derived Telemetry
item.description: Behind the Build is an ongoing series spotlighting standout Microsoft partner collaborations. Each edition dives into the technical and strategic decisions that shape real-world integrations—highlighting engineering excellence, innovation, and the shared customer value created through partnership....
item.dc:creator: Natee_Pretikul

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: New Powershell module: Az.PostgreSQLFlexibleServer
item.description: We’re excited to introduce the newly renamed Az.PostgreSQLFlexibleServer PowerShell module, delivering a more streamlined experience for you to manage Azure Database for PostgreSQL with PowerShell. Building on the capabilities of the previous Az.PostgreSq
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Document PII playground sample in Microsoft Foundry NextGen
item.description: Document-based PII detection in Azure AI Language now ships an initial playground experience in the Microsoft Foundry NextGen portal. The playground loads a prepared sample document, runs it through the document-based PII feature, and shows the redacted o
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Document PII NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description: Azure AI Language now offers a refreshed Document PII playground in general availability, giving customers a faster way to evaluate personally identifiable information detection on documents. The playground ships with curated sample inputs and outputs, so
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.127
item.description: Learn what is new in Visual Studio Code 1.127 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.128 (Insiders)
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.128 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Instant Access via application consistent restore points
item.description: Azure introduces Instant Access for VM Restore Points, enabling you to restore disks immediately after a restore point is created—without waiting for background data replication.Key benefits:Faster recovery (lower RTO): Restore disks instantly—no wait for
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover now supports migration from Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
item.description: Azure Storage Mover now supports cloud-to-cloud migration of data from Google Cloud Storage (GCS) to Azure Blob Storage, helping organizations simplify multi-cloud consolidation onto Azure.Using the S3-compatible interface, customers can migrate data from
item.dc:creator: Microsoft
